response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned, versatile engineer with strong web and systems experience, but the role requires deep, recent C++ expertise for industrial automation. Their professional C++ exposure is limited to a short stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015; the bulk of their career is TypeScript/Node.js and DLang web/backend work. There is no evidence of MQTT, message brokers at industrial scale, IEC 62443/SDL practices, or industrial-protocol experience, making the fit weak despite solid engineering fundamentals.
strengths: Experience with low-latency, high-throughput systems (async I/O, fibers, caching) at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
Strong software craftsmanship background: TDD, CI/CD, mentoring, workshops, and open-source contributions
Broad full-stack and cross-platform experience including embedded and event-driven architectures
concerns: Minimal professional C++/STL experience — roughly six months, and it dates back a decade
No industrial cybersecurity, SDL, or IEC 62443 exposure; no MQTT or industrial protocol experience
Career trajectory centers on web/product engineering, not automation machinery or networked multi-threaded C++ systems
